user.summary: Maya — ML researcher at NimbusLabs working on climate-forecasting world models. Ships hackathon side projects in parallel; writes essays on agents.

chips.label: ClimateMesh lead — 1.8M-param VICReg+pred world model
chips.raw: Lead researcher on ClimateMesh. The 1.8M-parameter VICReg+pred SOTA variant beats 100× larger weather foundation models on 5-day forecast retrieval. Phase 4 pre-paper as of 2026-04-30.

chips.label: Active: Diff-Forecast plan (Qwen-Sky SFT)
chips.raw: Active research plan Diff-Forecast — un-parked 2026-04-26. Pipeline reproduces the paper; SFT regression discovered, recipe sweep in progress before scaling. Trains Qwen-Sky on AWS.

chips.label: Active: SkyDiff E2 (diffusion-LM + LoRA)
chips.raw: SkyDiff E2 ablation status update 2026-04-29: both tracks positive. Track 1 v2→v3 reveals capacity tradeoff with full-FFN coverage; Track 2 is now a design-iteration story with positive control.

chips.label: Trains on AWS (4× A100) → W&B → HuggingFace
chips.raw: Compute stack: AWS GPUs (mostly 4× A100 for Phase 2 trajectory experiments), W&B for logging, HuggingFace for artifact persistence. Training runs are usually overnight.

chips.label: Manim style: 3Blue1Brown classic palette
chips.raw: Preferred Manim visualization style is 3Blue1Brown classic palette. Uses Manim CE for explainer animations on the team blog.

chips.label: North-star: "models anyone can hand to a meteorologist"
chips.raw: Long-term goal: build climate models that working meteorologists can use without an ML degree.

chips.label: Currently in: Hermes Creative Hackathon (deadline 2026-05-03)
chips.raw: Active hackathon entry: Hermes Agent Creative Hackathon (Nous Research × Kimi/Moonshot). $15k Main + $5k Kimi tracks. Deadline EOD Sun 2026-05-03.

chips.label: Writes mini-essays on agentic systems
chips.raw: MiniEssays series. "Agentic Tool Calling" (Dec 2025) — argues tool use is the most important pattern in modern agentic systems for going beyond knowledge cutoffs.
